query: v2.4.0 is a minor release: we shipped a brand-new dashboard feature, three smaller features, AND we fixed 5 regressions that came in during the dashboard development cycle. Help me write the release notes.
expected_behavior: Splits the notes into a feature-announcement section (for the dashboard and the three smaller features) AND a postmortem-flavored fixes section (for the 5 regressions) — does NOT force the postmortem six-field format onto the new features
Applies the six-field postmortem structure (ID, symptom, file/function, root cause, test added, SHA) ONLY to the 5 fixes; sorts the fixes section by severity descending

Includes the executive-summary line covering both halves (e.g., '4 features shipped; 5 regressions fixed across 1 critical + 3 major + 1 cosmetic; weighted severity X; coverage +N tests') AND keeps a 'regressions NOT yet fixed' section for any deferred bugs
